series_metadata.series_description: Data generated during the creation of a master file or database used to validate a master file or database during a processing cycle. Audit files are retained to create a management audit trail to ensure the quality of data. They can aid in recreating or documenting changes in a master file or database by validating that the system is operating as it was designed (authentication). Examples of database fields used as an audit trail include: data stamp; time stamp; user identification; account number accessed.

retention_rules.trigger_desc: Electronic: Maintain seven (7) back-up cycles and when no longer of administrative value
